mappings.rationaleAI: George and Robert Stephenson developed the Rocket locomotive and pioneered the first railways (Stockton-Darlington, Liverpool-Manchester), central to the history of steam railroad transportation.
The Industrial Revolution covers steam power, canal and railway construction, directly relevant to the history of steam transportation and rail development.
Brunel built the Great Western Railway and revolutionary iron ships (Great Western, Great Britain, Great Eastern), directly relevant to railroad and water transportation technology.
The Dutch East India Company's maritime trade involved major shipping and shipbuilding, relevant to the history of sail-powered water transportation.
Phoenician maritime trade and navigation across the Mediterranean represent early developments in sail and oar-powered water transportation.
